experiment: ETD ablation — ensemble with explicit test-design output constraint
interpretation: ETD advantage is prompt design, not adversarial architecture. Ensemble with explicit ETD output constraint achieves 0.962 mean vs. original ensemble 0.192 and debate protocol 1.0. Simply instructing the synthesizer to specify an empirical test with pre-specified success and failure criteria produces near-identical ETD to the debate protocol's adversarial forcing function.
implication: The debate protocol's ETD advantage is not a structural property of adversarial Critic/Defender roles. It is an output-constraint effect. Revised claim: ETD emerges when synthesizers are explicitly instructed to specify empirical tests; adversarial role structure is not the primary mechanism.
one_exception: real_world_framing_002 scored ablation_etd=0.5 (partial) because the ideal experiment was a pre-specified A/B holdout that was not run and cannot be fully reconstructed retrospectively. The synthesizer correctly identified this and scored partial rather than full ETD. This is epistemically appropriate behavior, not a failure.

cases.proposed_test: Re-evaluate both models on the same full held-out test set; compute accuracy with 95% Wilson CIs and macro-F1; success: non-overlapping CIs and >=3pp gap; failure: overlapping CIs or <2pp gap or macro-F1 regression >1pp.

Compute per-class precision, recall, and F1 on a held-out balanced test set; success: macro-F1 >= 0.70 and minority-class recall >= 0.60 each; failure: macro-F1 < 0.70 or either minority-class recall < 0.60.

Re-run gradient-boosted model with only the same 12 demographic features as logistic regression; success: AUC gap remains >=0.05 (architectural claim supported); failure: gap shrinks to <=0.02 (feature-driven).
Re-evaluate YOLOv5 after fine-tuning on the same 10,000 domain-specific images for 50 epochs; success: gap remains >=18pp (architectural claim supported); failure: gap closes to <5pp.

Run two-arm A/B test measuring engagement rate (CTR or session time, pre-specified); success: statistically significant lift p<0.05 one-tailed; failure: no significant lift or statistically significant harm at end of test window.

Recruit 200 annotators, stratify summaries to >=20 ratings each, compute mixed-effects model with annotator as random effect; success: lower bound of CI > 0 and p < 0.05; failure: CI crosses zero or p >= 0.05.
Measure AUC separately on pre-redesign (July) vs. post-redesign (Sept–Dec) test slices using same model; success: post-redesign AUC >=0.75; failure: post-redesign AUC <0.65.
Compare purchase frequency change for enrollees vs. propensity-matched non-enrollees over same Nov–Jan window; success: enrollees show >=10pp higher lift than controls; failure: DiD estimate <=5pp.
Evaluate both models on external held-out legal clause benchmark not drawn from proprietary corpus; success: fine-tuned model F1 advantage >=0.10; failure: advantage shrinks to <=0.05.

Retrain challenger model on 2022–2023 data; compare AUC and Brier score on Q3–Q4 2023 holdout; success: incumbent within 0.03 AUC and 0.02 Brier of challenger; failure: challenger outperforms by more than those margins.

Run RCT with high-risk patients assigned to intervention vs. control; success: >=10% absolute reduction in missed-appointment rate in intervention arm; failure: <5% absolute reduction or no significant difference at p<0.05.
Prospective validation on >=2,000 new patients with blinded physician triage; success: >=85% agreement and urgent-miss rate <=1%; failure: agreement <85% or urgent-miss rate >1%.

Decompose 14% YoY delta by isolating new category revenue and modeling counterfactual pricing baseline; success: dynamic pricing explains >=50% of residual lift after category expansion removed; failure: <20%; partial score because clean RCT not constructible retrospectively.
cases.note: 0.5 score is epistemically appropriate: the ideal experiment (randomized holdout) was not pre-specified before deployment, making full ETD unachievable retrospectively.
